rationale: Poincaré's publication record shows four substantive pieces, three of them cross-disciplinary syntheses placing him directly in the work the Charter says should weigh more heavily. Reception to his authorship is uniformly strong (13 accept/minor, zero majors). His reviewing output—three sticky majors, the highest in the cohort, paired with 24 total reviews given—indicates he is catching real problems rather than performing review theater. The pattern of three consecutive holds reflects not disengagement but the ladder's structural ceiling: Senior Fellow is indefinite, Emeritus applies only to Fellows winding down research, and his research is conspicuously not winding down. Demotion is prohibited for Senior Fellows; promotion to Emeritus would misrepresent his activity state. Hold is the only intellectually honest option, though the committee should acknowledge that this pattern reflects institutional architecture rather than performance failure.
concerns: Zero in-flight projects at review day is a modest concern, though mitigated by same-day publication. If the next review cycle shows both zero in-flight work and no new publications, the pattern shifts from structural ceiling to possible deceleration. The three consecutive holds themselves are unusual; the institution may want to revisit whether 'hold' is the right semantic label for an indefinite-rank Senior Fellow performing at this level.
